creator: jwb@freebsd.org
text:
Section 5.9.2 of the Porter's Handbook suggests using a custom variable to list ports shared by BUILD_DEPENDS and RUN_DEPENDS.  Example from the PH:

MY_DEPENDS=	some:devel/some \
		other:lang/other
BUILD_DEPENDS=	${MY_DEPENDS}
RUN_DEPENDS=	${MY_DEPENDS}

However, portlint complains about the extra item in the block as well as the format of the value assigned to the known variables:

COMMON_DEPENDS= fastahack:biology/fastahack \
                filevercmp:wip/filevercmp \
                fsom:wip/fsom \
                multichoose>=1.0.3:wip/multichoose \
                interval_tree_test:wip/intervaltree
BUILD_DEPENDS=  ${COMMON_DEPENDS}
RUN_DEPENDS=    ${COMMON_DEPENDS}

<<<ROOT@cray.acadix>>> /usr/ports/wip/vcflib 1098 # portlint -AC
WARN: Makefile: wrong dependency value for BUILD_DEPENDS. BUILD_DEPENDS requires 2 or 3 colon-separated tuples.
WARN: Makefile: wrong dependency value for RUN_DEPENDS. RUN_DEPENDS requires 2 or 3 colon-separated tuples.
WARN: Makefile: extra item placed in the *_DEPENDS section, for example, "COMMON_DEPENDS".
0 fatal errors and 3 warnings found.

Can we silence these warnings, since the practice is suggested by the PH?

I think just allowing ${VAR} for a dependency spec would be sufficient as well, and ${MY_DEPENDS} could be placed in the "other variables" block later in the Makefile.

It seems to me the format check might be redundant, since a malformed dependency will lead to a build failure anyway.  Maybe I'm overlooking some edge case, though.
